mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.09% indicates deep accommodation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during periods of FEDFUNDS < 1.0% do not significantly exceed baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 200k signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 142,624k reflects ongoing employment levels
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE increase > 0.3pp) signals demand destruction and recessionary pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month decline > 1.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.3pp within 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 6.7% and RSAFS at 540,915M
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (both conditions met) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 1.0% month-over-month indicates expanding productive capacity and supply-side strength, supporting corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO > 1.0% growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1,400k units combined with falling real GDP growth signals demand weakness and forward economic contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1,400k AND GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth < 1.5% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1,547k and GDPC1 at 18,596.521B
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 3.5% year-over-year triggers expectations of sustained monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during high-inflation periods (CPI YoY > 3.5%) are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 7.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power, which depresses corporate earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market weakness or recessionary environment
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when UNRATE > 7.0% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales (RSAFS month-over-month > 1.0%) and housing starts (HOUST > 1,600k) indicates robust consumer and housing demand that sustains corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 540,915M and HOUST at 1,547k
regime_hint: demand-driven expansion with strong consumer and housing activity
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
